published_prior_art.cite: DaouiaFlorensSimar2010FrontierEVT: boundary-tail EVT algebra gives the analogue, but not for CCOT PRTE endpoints

repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest Stat catalogue entries are policy-regret overlap phase rates and dose-response minimax converses, not PRTE OT boundary experiments

published_prior_art.cite: FangSantos2019DirectionalInference gives generic directionally differentiable inference, but no CCOT PRTE propensity-boundary local experiment

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue PRTE OT endpoint limit law

published_prior_art.cite: TanBlanchetSyrgkanis2026PRTEOT gives continuous-IV rates; FranguridiLiu2025RegularizedOTPartialID gives entropic OT inference, not the unregularized boundary-tail limit law

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue honest-CI adaptation lower bound for PRTE OT endpoints

published_prior_art.cite: CaiLow2005AdaptiveCI and RobinsVanderVaart2006AdaptiveSets supply generic adaptation templates, not bounded-divergence alternatives inside CCOT PRTE
ImbensManski2004PartialCI and Stoye2009MorePartialCI already cover regular asymptotically normal interval endpoints; this is a CCOT strict-overlap sanity reduction
none; no verified fixed-alpha honest CI construction for the unregularized CCOT PRTE boundary-local limit law

soundness_flags.label: def:variance-proxy / prop:phase-normalization
soundness_flags.one_line: With V_P(t)=integral b^(-2) dT_P(b) and T_P(t) asymptotic to t^alpha, the stated alpha=1 phase algebra is wrong; the integral has a threshold at alpha=2 and may be infinite under the stated tail class.

soundness_flags.label: ass:nuisance-estimability
soundness_flags.one_line: SasakiUra2018PRTEInference supports orthogonal PRTE nuisance-rate logic, but not the boundary-rate-adapted condition s_n=o(r_n(P)^(-1)) as a standard assumption.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:active-face-gap / honest_scope
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The limit-law and CI claims are accepted only on the unique-active-face subclass; tied or slack active OT faces are explicitly out of scope.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: All headline inference claims are conditional on the unique-active-face gap and do not cover competing-face or tie regimes.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Fix the boundary variance proxy or the phase-rate statement so the Stieltjes tail calculation actually yields the claimed alpha=1 transition.
Retag ass:nuisance-estimability as a novel rate condition with a realistic justification, or weaken the endpoint theorem to carry an explicit nuisance remainder instead of assuming it away.
Add a positive fixed-alpha CI theorem or sharper construction target alongside the no-adaptation lower bound, even if the current CI remains an open-ended question.
Add exact location anchors for TanBlanchetSyrgkanis2026PRTEOT continuous-IV rate results and for the Imbens-Manski-Stoye regular endpoint template.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: repair the boundary-normalization algebra and the nuisance-rate assumption tag, then rerun novelty review.
